name: Varo Bank, National Association
description_short: Varo is a mobile-first national bank offering fee-free checking, high-yield savings up to 5.00% APY, and credit-building tools without hidden fees.
description_long:
Varo Bank, National Association is a federally chartered national bank operating as a digital-only institution focused on serving consumers who want transparent, low-cost banking. The company emphasizes eliminating surprise fees and offers a full suite of banking products delivered exclusively through mobile and web platforms.

Varo's core offerings include a 100% free checking account with no overdraft, minimum balance, or in-network ATM fees; a high-yield savings account paying up to 5.00% APY on balances up to $5,000 and 2.50% APY on amounts above that; cash advances up to $250 (expandable to $500 with direct deposit) with flat fees ranging from $1.60–$40; a line of credit up to $2,000; and the Varo Believe Visa Credit Card designed for credit building. Additional features include early direct deposit (up to 2 days early), fee-free cash deposits at 7,500+ CVS locations, cashback offers on everyday purchases, and free credit score tracking in the mobile app.

Varo distinguishes itself through its explicit no-hidden-fees positioning, substantial APY rates relative to traditional banks, and integration of credit-building tools into the core banking experience. The company reports strong customer satisfaction (4.9 stars on App Store, 4.7 on Google Play with 216k and 230k reviews respectively) and claims that Believe card users see an average 40+ point credit score increase within 3 months of on-time payments. Deposits are FDIC-insured up to $250,000, and the company holds its own banking charter.

Varo is well-suited for digitally-native consumers seeking transparent banking without fees, but users should be aware that while in-network ATM withdrawals are free, out-of-network withdrawals and cash deposits carry fees. The cash advance and line of credit products, while competitive, still involve costs and qualification requirements. The platform's digital-only model means no physical branch access. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Fee-free checking account (no overdraft, minimum balance, or in-network ATM fees)
High-yield savings account with tiered APY (5.00% up to $5,000; 2.50% above)
Cash advances up to $250 ($500 with direct deposit) with flat fees and no interest
Line of credit up to $2,000 with transparent pricing and no hidden fees
Varo Believe Visa Credit Card for credit building on everyday purchases
Early direct deposit (up to 2 days early)
Fee-free cash deposits at CVS locations
Cashback offers on gas, groceries, dining, and other categories
Free credit score tracking and monitoring in mobile app
Early tax refund feature (up to 5 days early)
pros: High-yield savings rate of 5.00% APY on up to $5,000 (one of the highest in the market per their claims)
Genuinely no overdraft fees, no minimum balance fees, and no in-network ATM fees across all accounts
Cash advances up to $500 with flat, transparent fees ($1.60–$40) and no interest charges
Free cash deposits at 7,500+ CVS locations nationwide, solving a major pain point for digital banks
Early direct deposit (up to 2 days) reduces time to access paychecks
Varo Believe Visa Credit Card with reported average 40+ point credit score increase in 3 months for on-time payers
FDIC insurance up to $250,000 and federally chartered status provides institutional credibility
cons: Out-of-network ATM withdrawals and cash deposits incur fees (not specified on homepage, creating lack of transparency)
Digital-only banking model means no physical branches for in-person support or transactions
Cash advance and line of credit products require qualification and involve fees; not available to all applicants
High APY rate (5.00%) only applies to up to $5,000; balances above that earn significantly less (2.50%)
Line of credit up to $2,000 is relatively modest compared to traditional personal loans

contact.address: 11781 S Lone Peak Pkwy, Draper, UT 84020

best_for: Tech-savvy consumers who prefer mobile banking and don't need physical branch access
Savers looking to maximize APY on deposits under $5,000 with a low-cost, transparent bank
Individuals building or rebuilding credit who want to combine banking with credit-building products
People seeking emergency cash advances or credit lines with transparent, flat-fee pricing
diagnosis: Varo is ideal for digitally-native, savings-focused consumers who want transparent banking without surprise fees and are interested in building credit. The main caveat is that the bank is entirely digital with no physical branches, and the headline 5.00% APY only applies to the first $5,000 of savings; out-of-network ATM and cash deposit fees apply despite the 'no hidden fees' messaging.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
address: 11781 S Lone Peak Pkwy, Draper, UT 84020
